title: Making equal groups for 4 / 2

segments.narration: Here are 4 apples altogether. We want to put them into equal groups of 2.
segments.visualCue: Show all 4 apples in one collection before any grouping begins.

segments.narration: We keep making groups of 2. Each time we make one full group, we count it. 4 divided by 2 tells how many groups we can form.
segments.visualCue: Move the apples into equal groups of 2 and count each completed group.

segments.narration: Let's group them together. We can make 2 full groups of 2, so the answer is 2.
segments.visualCue: Highlight each finished group and show the number of groups growing to 2.

segments.narration: Quick check: if 4 apples are grouped into sets of 2, how many groups do we make? 2.
segments.visualCue: Show the grouped apples beside the equation 4 ÷ 2 = 2.

segments.narration: Great work. Division helped us make 2 equal groups of 2, so 4 divided by 2 equals 2.
segments.visualCue: Hold the final equal groups on screen and celebrate the quotient 2.

teacherNotes: Have students say the total, the size of each group, and the number of groups aloud as the apples are sorted.
